ucs.title: [LLM] APT40 (Leviathan) spearphish chain: maldoc → WMI/PowerShell exec → suspicious BITS staging
ucs.description: Hunts Leviathan/APT40's signature initial access pattern: a maritime/defense-themed spearphishing attachment (T1566.001) executed by the user (T1204.002), spawning Office → WMI/PowerShell child processes (T1059.001, T1047) followed by BITS-based payload retrieval (T1197) and DLL-side-loaded second-stage (T1574.002 / T1218). This combination of Office → wmic/powershell → bitsadmin within a short window is highly characteristic of MUDCARP/TEMP.Periscope intrusions documented by CISA AA21-200A and Mandiant.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as processes values(Processes.parent_process) as parents values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe") Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wmic.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name _time span=1h
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| join type=inner dest [
    | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="bitsadmin.exe" (Processes.process="*transfer*" OR Processes.process="*addfile*" OR Processes.process="*http*") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process _time span=1h
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
    | rename count as bits_count process as bits_cmd parent_process_name as bits_parent
  ]
| where bits_count >= 1
| eval risk_score = count + (bits_count*5)
| where risk_score >= 6
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` 
| table firstTime dest user parent_process_name process_name processes bits_parent bits_cmd risk_score
ucs.kql:
let officeChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wmic.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe"]);
let officeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe"]);
let stage1 = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (officeParents)
| where FileName in~ (officeChildren)
| project s1Time=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, s1Parent=InitiatingProcessFileName, s1Child=FileName, s1Cmd=ProcessCommandLine;
let stage2 = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName =~ "bitsadmin.exe" or (FileName =~ "powershell.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Start-BitsTransfer","BITS"))
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","transfer","addfile")
| project s2Time=Timestamp, DeviceId, s2Cmd=ProcessCommandLine, s2Parent=InitiatingProcessFileName;
stage1
| join kind=inner stage2 on DeviceId
| where s2Time between (s1Time .. s1Time + 1h)
| extend chain = strcat(s1Parent, " -> ", s1Child, " -> bits")
| summarize firstSeen=min(s1Time), lastSeen=max(s2Time), commands=make_set(pack("office",s1Cmd,"bits",s2Cmd),10) by DeviceName, AccountName, chain
| order by firstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: CISA AA21-200A and Mandiant reporting on TEMP.Periscope/APT40 explicitly call out the Office-macro → WMI/PowerShell → BITS staging chain (used to drop AIRBREAK/MURKYTOP/HOMEFRY against maritime, defense, and academic targets). The narrow 1-hour pivot window plus the BITS leg differentiates this from generic phishing-detection logic.

ucs.title: [LLM] APT40 web-shell + scheduled-task persistence on Internet-facing servers (CHINACHOPPER/PIPEMON pattern)
ucs.description: Hunts Leviathan's signature post-exploitation pattern after Exploit-Public-Facing-Application (T1190) intrusions on Exchange/Confluence/Citrix: w3wp.exe / java / tomcat spawning cmd/powershell (web shell behaviour, T1505.003) followed shortly by scheduled-task creation (T1053.005) and credential dumping via comsvcs/rundll32 minidump (T1003.001). APT40 has been repeatedly observed using CHINACHOPPER-style ASPX shells alongside this exact lateral pivot.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as child_procs from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat*.exe","java.exe","nginx.exe") Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","schtasks.exe","rundll32.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_name Processes.user _time span=30m
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| eval webshell_score = mvcount(split(cmdlines,"\n"))
| join type=inner dest [
    | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="schtasks.exe" Processes.process="*/create*") OR (Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" Processes.process="*comsvcs*MiniDump*") OR (Processes.process_name="powershell.exe" Processes.process="*lsass*") by Processes.dest Processes.process Processes.process_name _time span=30m
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
    | stats values(process) as followon_cmds count as followon_count by dest
  ]
| where followon_count >= 1
| table firstTime dest user parent_process_name child_procs cmdlines followon_cmds followon_count
| sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
let webProcs = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","java.exe","nginx.exe"]);
let sus = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","schtasks.exe","rundll32.exe","certutil.exe"]);
let webshell = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (webProcs)
| where FileName in~ (sus)
| project wsTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, wsParent=InitiatingProcessFileName, wsChild=FileName, wsCmd=ProcessCommandLine, AccountName;
let followon = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where (FileName =~ "schtasks.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "/create")
   or (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("comsvcs","MiniDump"))
   or (FileName =~ "powershell.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("lsass","Out-Minidump","Invoke-Mimikatz"))
   or (FileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe")
| project foTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, foCmd=ProcessCommandLine, foFile=FileName;
webshell
| join kind=inner followon on DeviceId
| where foTime between (wsTime .. wsTime + 30m)
| extend chain = strcat(wsParent, " -> ", wsChild, " -> ", foFile)
| summarize firstSeen=min(wsTime), lastSeen=max(foTime), webshell_cmds=make_set(wsCmd,10), followon_cmds=make_set(foCmd,10) by DeviceName, AccountName, chain
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FolderPath has_any ("\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\","\\webapps\\","\\Exchange Server\\","\\OWA\\","\\Confluence\\")
    | where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".jsp" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".asp"
    | summarize webshell_files=make_set(FolderPath,10) by DeviceName
  ) on DeviceName
| order by firstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: APT40/Leviathan (BRONZE MOHAWK, Kryptonite Panda) is documented by CISA, ACSC, NCSC and CrowdStrike for exploiting public-facing apps (Exchange ProxyLogon/ProxyShell, Atlassian Confluence, Citrix) to drop CHINACHOPPER-family ASPX/JSP shells, then immediately staging schtasks persistence and comsvcs/LSASS dumping for credential access. The w3wp/tomcat→shell + 30-min schtasks/lsass pivot is the acto
